pages.full_text:
next to where people - lots of fat people - are picnicking by the river.

[photo: Dartmeet postcard]

Dartmeet, East Dart River, modern bridge + ancient clapper bridge (600 years old ?) Dartmoor
photo_captions: Dartmeet, East Dart River, modern bridge + ancient clapper bridge (600 years old ?) Dartmoor
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in postcard photograph labelled 'DARTMEET' with a handwritten caption written rotated alongside the photo. No handwritten page number visible at top-left. Text at top is continuation from previous page.

pages.full_text:
We walked to where the 2 Darts meet. It's a marvellous place for children, clapper bridge, wet suits, rubber boats going through rapids, tiddle nets.

	We picked up Margaret + Helen + headed for home (forgetting the logan berry bushes that Evelyn had got ready for us.)

__July 1st, Saturday__
	After a mission planning day in church we left to pick up Margaret + Helen for supper in the van. A foot ball match was on TV so at 4.15. the roads were nearly empty. Very hot.
	We had a good evening at Hampton Lode. England lost to Portugal.
__July 22nd, Saturday.__ (Simon took the van to get lampshades at Blackburn)
	Charles, Peter, Simon, Eve + Tonic drove to Trimley where we gave Margaret + Helen lunch in the garden. Peter took along
pages.notes: Page number 39 visible at top-right. Text is continuation from left page (starts mid-sentence about the Dart rivers meeting at Dartmeet). 'tiddle nets' may be 'tiddler nets' — transcribed as written. 'Tonic' is likely a nickname or pet name. Year 2006 inferred because July 1st fell on a Saturday in 2006 and July 22nd 2006 was also a Saturday, consistent with both date mentions. Annotation '(Simon took the van to get lampshades at Blackburn)' appears as an interlinear note beside the July 22nd heading. Text ends mid-sentence at page bottom — continues on next page. 'logan berry' transcribed as written (loganberry).
